title: Expressions, Decisions, and Iteration · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Route support cases through explicit rules and iterate changing record sets without truthiness or termination defects. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a transparent support-case routing engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict expression grouping, short-circuit evaluation, assignment, optional chaining, and nullish coalescing.
Use truthiness only when every falsy value shares the same domain meaning and otherwise write explicit guards.
Express mutually exclusive branches, guard clauses, and exhaustive fallbacks without unreachable paths.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Operator Precedence · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Route tickets with priority 2, role "viewer", and maintenance=false through an explicit decision table. Then flip maintenance to true and prove exactly one branch owns the changed outcome.

Which action demonstrates Operator Precedence without dropping earlier contracts?

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
